summary: Full e2e tested NEXUS SaaS — backend 19/19 pytest passing (health, JWT auth, RBAC, API keys w/ X-API-Key auth, leads CRUD + stats + filter, OSINT DNS/IP/phone, AI graceful degradation, logout). Frontend tested via Playwright: login → dashboard → Overview stats → Lead Engine list/search/CSV/Enrich → OSINT DNS execution (real DNS records returned) → NEXUS AI chat (graceful) → API Keys generate+reveal (nxs_ prefix) → Admin operators list → logout. All flows green.
critical_code_review_comments: AI graceful-degradation endpoints (/api/enrichment/chat and /enrichment/enrich) return HTTP 200 with {error: ...} as designed since HF_TOKEN is intentionally invalid; behavior matches spec.
Lead search input requires Enter or magnifier-icon click to trigger filtering (no live debounce). This is a UX choice, not a bug — flagging only in case live-search is desired.
/api/leads/{id}/sell takes price as query param (not body) — works fine but inconsistent with REST style. Low priority.
Refresh-token cookie issued but no /auth/refresh endpoint exists; access token will expire in 12h and users will need to re-login. Consider adding refresh flow.
Indexes: only users.email is indexed. For scale, consider indexes on leads.category, leads.status, leads.score and api_keys.key_hash. Not blocking.
test_credentials: admin@nexus.io / nexus123 (seeded on startup). Non-admin users register via POST /api/auth/register with non-reserved email domains (e.g. @example.com — pydantic EmailStr rejects .local).
seed_data_creation: Test creates ephemeral users (TEST_*@example.com) and TEST_ leads which are deleted within their test; one revoked API key (TEST_key) remains in DB but is harmless (revoked=True).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — 19 tests, ~5s runtime. Uses session-scoped admin login + a fresh registered non-admin user for RBAC. HF_TOKEN is intentionally invalid; /enrichment/* returning {error:...} with 200 is EXPECTED. Lead search in UI does not live-filter; must press Enter or click search-icon button.
